topic: OpenAI Agents SDK Tutorial 2026: Build Multi-Agent Pipelines in Python

competitors.title: OpenAI Agents SDK Tutorial: Build Production AI Agents
key_points: OpenAI Agents SDK provides production-grade primitives: Agents, Handoffs, Guardrails, and Tracing
Agents are configuration objects wrapping LLMs with instructions, tools, and output schemas
Handoffs enable multi-agent orchestration - agents can transfer control to specialized agents
Guardrails provide input/output validation for safety and quality control
The SDK is model-agnostic - supports OpenAI or any provider with chat completions API format
Structured outputs via Pydantic models enable predictable data flow between agents
Tracing captures every step for observability and debugging
Supports fast deployment with minimal boilerplate compared to other frameworks

competitors.title: Build Your First Multi-Agent System with OpenAI Agents SDK — Step-by-Step Python Tutorial (2026)
key_points: Complete three-agent pipeline tutorial: Research Agent → Writer Agent → Reviewer Agent
Two orchestration patterns: Handoffs (delegation chain) vs Agents-as-tools (orchestrator pattern)
Real cost breakdown: ~$0.003 per pipeline run with gpt-4o-mini
Detailed cost optimization strategies: caching, structured outputs, model selection
Comparison with alternatives: LangGraph (graph-based) and CrewAI (role-based)
Pragmatic Python implementation under 100 lines of core logic
Production-ready patterns: dynamic instructions, parallel execution, agent cloning
Common pitfalls and solutions: infinite loops, vague handoffs, unstructured data
